_comment: Primary evidence source for pc-cleaner decisions on non-technical machines. Presented as ONE AskUserQuestion call with 4 multi-select questions, ~15 checkboxes total. Each checkbox maps to a set of consequences in the unified plan. Forensic signals (UserAssist, Prefetch, file associations) become CONFIRMATION only — if a checkbox is unticked but forensic signals show recent usage, KEEP wins. Default when a signal is missing: KEEP (don't remove things the user didn't explicitly deprioritize). Added 2026-07-07 after user pointed out that autostart / file associations reflect OEM defaults, not deliberate choices, on non-technical machines.

questions.prompt: What do you use this laptop for regularly? (Tick everything that applies.)

options.label: Web browsing, email, YouTube, social media
Documents, spreadsheets, video calls (Word, Excel, Zoom, Teams)

keep: Windows Print Spooler if a printer is configured

options.label: Photos, music, or video editing / creation
Games (Steam, Epic, Xbox app, PC games)

questions.prompt: What do you regularly connect to this PC? (Tick everything you use.)

options.label: Bluetooth headphones, speakers, mouse, keyboard, or earbuds

keep: fdPHost + FDResPub (WSD network printers) — already tripwire

options.label: A phone — Phone Link, photo transfer, backup

keep: Apple Mobile Device Service if iPhone

options.label: I cast my screen or audio wirelessly to a TV / speaker (Miracast, DLNA)

keep: SSDPSRV + upnphost + WFDSConMgrSvc (all tripwire) — cast pipeline

questions.prompt: Which of these apply to you? (Tick everything true.)

options.label: I sign into this PC with a Microsoft account (or use Outlook.com, Office 365)

keep: built-in Mail / Calendar UWP if used

options.label: I use OneDrive to sync files
I use Microsoft Teams for work

keep: TaskbarMn (Chat icon) or the Teams pin
webthreatdefusersvc (Teams uses it for link safety)

options.label: I take screenshots or record my screen with Windows key + G (Xbox Game Bar)

implies.note: Explicit signal that Game Bar backs a used feature — overrides gamer-signal inference

questions.prompt: How do you like Windows to feel? (Tick everything that matches.)

options.label: I type in the Start menu to find files and open apps

implies.note: Keeps BingSearchEnabled decision as 'ask' vs silent-disable

options.label: I prefer dark mode for Windows and apps

implies.note: Direct evidence — no inference needed; taste question resolved

options.label: I use virtual desktops (Win+Tab or the taskbar squares icon) to organize windows

implies.note: Direct evidence — the previously ask-gated Task View button decision is resolved

options.label: I use (or want to try) Windows Recall — the AI feature that takes screenshots for search

implies.note: Direct evidence — resolves the Recall ask_user gate

inference_rules.unticked_default: KEEP — an unticked box means 'not confirmed', not 'hate it'. Only remove/disable when BOTH (a) checkbox unticked AND (b) forensic signal shows zero recent usage (UserAssist count = 0 AND Prefetch shows no launch in 90 days) AND (c) the item is not in tripwire.
inference_rules.forensic_override: If a checkbox is unticked BUT UserAssist or Prefetch shows recent usage, TRUST the forensic signal (KEEP). User may have forgotten to tick; can't be wrong about the count.
inference_rules.tripwire_wins: Any service in data/services_tripwire.json stays regardless of checkbox state. Checkboxes affect the ask_user category items and the bloat / cleanup / UI-tweak buckets — never override tripwire.
inference_rules.hardware_still_gates: Skip conditions from module docs still apply. If Get-PnpDevice shows no biometric hardware, don't apply Windows-Hello-related tweaks even if 'I sign into MS account' is ticked.

coverage_target: These 16 checkboxes cover the decisions that determine ~85% of what pc-cleaner touches on a typical machine. Anything not covered gets Claude's usage-based inference (from services.md / bloat.md etc.) with KEEP as the tiebreaker.
